title: Composition of injective functions is injective
informal_statement: If f : alpha -> beta and g : beta -> gamma are both injective functions, then their composition g . f : alpha -> gamma is injective.
informal_proof_idea: Unfold injectivity directly: to show g . f is injective, take a, b with (g . f) a = (g . f) b, i.e. g (f a) = g (f b). Injectivity of g gives f a = f b, then injectivity of f gives a = b.
formal_statement_pp: theorem injective_comp : ∀ {α β γ : Type} (f : α → β) (g : β → γ), Function.Injective f → Function.Injective g → Function.Injective (g ∘ f)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode ab41c15c). Step 1 kernel-failed on a binder-arity slip (forgot to `intro` the three implicit type variables alpha/beta/gamma before the value/hypothesis arguments); step 2 fixed the intro list and reached kernel_verified. Not packaged as a separate negative_example: the failure is a plain authoring slip (miscounted binders), not a tactic-mismatch or automation hazard with independent training value, unlike e.g. the pow_succ/omega negative examples elsewhere in the corpus. Fills a real content gap in packets/elementary/functions/: all 16 prior packets in this domain were abs/max/min identities despite the domain's stated focus (injective/surjective/composition/inverse/monotone/fixed-point basics) having zero prior coverage; this is the first packet on that stated focus.
